From Step 1, increasing volume from 10.0 to 20.0 (factor of 2.0) while keeping density and k constant caused mass to increase from 90.0 to 180.0 (factor of 2.0). This suggests mass is directly proportional to volume. From Step 2, increasing k from 3.0 to 4.0 (factor of 4/3) while keeping density and volume constant caused mass to increase from 180.0 to 240.0 (factor of 4/3). This suggests mass is directly proportional to k. Therefore, mass = density * volume * k. I have sufficient evidence to conclude the equation.
